protocol_name: Broth/Agar Dilution Antimicrobial Susceptibility Testing for Aerobic Bacteria
paper_source: CLSI document M07-A10: Methods for Dilution Antimicrobial Susceptibility Tests for Bacteria That Grow Aerobically; Approved Standard—Tenth Edition

sequential_steps.notes: Prepare a series of tubes or plates with broth or agar medium containing various concentrations of antimicrobial agents.
Standardize the test organism suspension before inoculation.
Inoculate the prepared broth tubes or agar plates with the standardized suspension of the test organism.
Incubate at 35°C ± 2°C. Exact incubation duration is not stated in the provided excerpt.
Examine the tests after incubation and determine the MIC as the lowest concentration preventing visible growth.
